final_text:
Let's calculate the intrinsic carrier concentration.

For an intrinsic semiconductor, electrical conductivity is:
$$\sigma = n_i e (\mu_e + \mu_h)$$

Where:
- $\sigma = 10^{-6} \, \Omega^{-1} \text{m}^{-1}$
- $e = 1.602 \times 10^{-19} \, \text{C}$
- $\mu_e = 0.75 \, \text{m}^2\text{V}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1}$
- $\mu_h = 0.06 \, \text{m}^2\text{V}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1}$

$$n_i = \frac{\sigma}{e(\mu_e + \mu_h)} = \frac{10^{-6}}{1.602 \times 10^{-19} \times (0.75 + 0.06)}$$

$$\mu_e + \mu_h = 0.75 + 0.06 = 0.81$$

$$n_i = \frac{10^{-6}}{1.602 \times 10^{-19} \times 0.81}$$

$$n_i = \frac{10^{-6}}{1.2976 \times 10^{-19}} = 7.71 \times 10^{12} \, \text{m}^{-3}$$

This corresponds to option (B) $7.7 \times 10^{12}$.

[ANSWER]B[/ANSWER]
